en.extract:
The Aberdare cisticola (Cisticola aberdare) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is endemic to Kenya.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical high-elevation grassland.
It is threatened by habitat loss.


Description
The Aberdare cisticola is 12–15 cm (4.7–5.9 in) in length, and weighs 18–24 g (0.63–0.85 oz). It is a large and stocky cisticola with a heavy bill, plain face, boldly striped upperparts, and a medium-long graduated tail. Its voice is a mixture of peeuu tew tew and other short trills.


Diet
It feeds on beetles and flies, foraging for them by searching through vegetation and on the ground.


Behavior
The Aberdare cisticola is believed to be monogamous, solitary and territorial. It breeds from January through May and August through November, building a nest out of a flimsy ball of branches and leaves.

The '''Aberdare cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola aberdare''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Kenya]].

Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] high-elevation [[grassland]].
It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name=":0">{{cite iucn |author1=BirdLife International |date=2018 |title=''Cisticola aberdare'' |volume=2018 |article-number=e.T22713436A129880372 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T22713436A129880372.en |access-date=6 September 2021}}</ref>

== Description ==
The Aberdare cisticola is {{cvt|12|-|15|cm|in|abbr=on}} in length, and weighs {{cvt|18|-|24|g|oz|abbr=on}}. It is a large and stocky [[cisticola]] with a heavy bill, plain face, boldly striped upperparts, and a medium-long graduated tail.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|url=https://www.hbw.com/species/aberdare-cisticola-cisticola-aberdare|title=Aberdare Cisticola (Cisticola aberdare)|website=www.hbw.com|language=en|access-date=2018-02-21}}</ref> Its voice is a mixture of ''peeuu tew tew'' and other short trills.<ref name=":0" />

== Diet ==
It feeds on [[beetle]]s and [[Fly|flies]], foraging for them by searching through vegetation and on the ground.<ref name=":1" />

== Behavior ==
The Aberdare cisticola<ref>{{Cite web |last=Aditya |first=Varun |date=2025-07-12 |title=Aberdare Cisticola |url=https://birdspedia.org/birds/aberdare-cisticola/ |access-date=2025-07-13 |website=BirdsPedia.org |language=en-US}}</ref> is believed to be monogamous, solitary and territorial. It breeds from January through May and August through November, building a nest out of a flimsy ball of branches and leaves.<ref name=":1" />
